decision.reason: P0-93 passed only on locked oracle smoke. The dev split provides 493 MoE records with low overlap against the locked smoke path and full expected_json scene graph supervision; real_world_benchmark_v3 smoke provides a smaller model-output-adjacent sanity path.

pros: Can reuse fusion_v2 + P0-93 replay wrapper directly.
cons: Still CubiCasa/SVG-derived oracle supervision, not real raster model-output validation.
Small overlap with locked smoke must be reported.

pros: Different smoke path and original image naming; useful sanity check.
cons: Still expected_json supervised; not sufficient alone for publication claim.

pros: Exercises registered expert output path rather than pure oracle predictions.
cons: Already fused output; applying contains policy requires a graph-output wrapper and careful gold alignment.
Same locked smoke family; not enough as independent validation.
No direct expected_json scene_graph contract for immediate fusion_v2 relation replay.

primary: Compare baseline vs center_ratio_1_top4 relation F1, contains precision/recall, missing/extra contains.
Report overlap count with locked smoke and avoid claiming full cross-dataset generalization.
secondary: Run same replay on datasets/cadstruct_real_world_benchmark_v3/smoke.jsonl as a sanity check.
If primary and secondary both pass, package as validation-positive opt-in candidate but still avoid default runtime promotion until model-output validation.
